channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Cuba Faces Energy Crisis Amid US Sanctions and International Support Efforts
item.description: Cuba is currently facing a severe energy crisis due to prolonged U.S. sanctions and a targeted blockade that has disrupted oil imports from key suppliers like Venezuela and Mexico. The island nation r...

item.title: Alas Pilipinas players push for better athlete welfare as PNVF vows reform
item.description: Ang mga manlalaro ng Alas Pilipinas, partikular na si kapitan Jia de Guzman at si Fifi Sharma, ay nagpahayag ng mga alalahanin laban sa kondisyon ng Philippine National Volleyball Federation (PNVF) at...

item.title: Global Celebrations of Eidul Fitr Amid Challenges in Conflict Zones
item.description: Muslims across the world observed Eidul Fitr for the year 1447 AH, marking the end of the holy month of Ramazan. While Eid is traditionally celebrated with prayers, feasting, and gift-giving, the mood...

item.title: Canada’s Einarson Advances to World Curling Semifinals After Defeating Japan
item.description: Kerri Einarson and her Canadian curling team secured a place in the semifinals of the 2026 World Curling Championship after a hard-fought 6-5 victory over Japan. The Canadians, led by Einarson, ended ...

item.title: Two people charged after alleged attempt to access Faslane naval base in Scotland
item.description: An Iranian man and a Romanian woman have been charged following an alleged attempt to gain unauthorised access to the Faslane naval base in Scotland, officially known as HM Naval Base Clyde. The incid...

item.title: Tanzanian experiences shape American's view on the importance of travel and cross-cultural understanding
item.description: Victoria Nguyen, an American woman, has developed a deep connection with Tanzania after visiting multiple times, despite the strict visa restrictions imposed on Tanzanians visiting the United States. ...

item.title: Anthropic and Pentagon Dispute Claims Over AI Tech and National Security in New Court Filing
item.description: Anthropic, an AI company, filed two sworn declarations in federal court in California, responding to the Pentagon's accusations that the company poses an unacceptable national security risk. The decla...

item.title: New Brunswick to Privatize Large Animal Veterinary Services, Raising Concern Among Farmers
item.description: Farmers across New Brunswick are voicing strong opposition to the provincial government’s decision to privatize its large animal veterinarian service, a program that has long been vital for rural comm...

item.title: Belgian Court Orders Trial of 93-Year-Old Diplomat for Role in Lumumba's 1961 Assassination
item.description: A Brussels court has ordered 93-year-old Etienne Davignon, former Belgian diplomat, to stand trial for war crimes related to the 1961 assassination of Patrice Lumumba, Congo's first prime minister aft...

item.title: Pastor Kanyari Explains His Pastoring Journey: Freebies and a 'Not-So-Divine' Calling
item.description: Controversial Kenyan pastor Victor Kanyari has stirred up a storm online after admitting that it wasn’t a divine calling that led him into ministry, but rather the allure of free gifts his mother rece...

item.title: How a simple greeting can make someone feel unsafe: a gendered look at Kenyan spaces
item.description: The article tells a personal story of how a simple greeting from a man changed how a woman views everyday interactions in public spaces in Kenya. The writer describes being greeted in a friendly tone,...

item.title: Borno government dey track remaining suicide bombers after Maiduguri blasts
item.description: Borno State governor, Babagana Zulum, don confirm say five suicide bombers enter Maiduguri, but only three don detonate their explosives so far. The attacks happen on Monday evening for three location...

item.title: The Waratahs' missed chances hand victory to the Blues
item.description: In his column, Peter FitzSimons reflects on the New South Wales Waratahs’ frustrating loss to the Auckland Blues in Super Rugby. Despite leading 17-8 at halftime and dominating possession and territor...

item.title: Toronto Man Wrongfully Accused of Murder Faces Renewed Scrutiny
item.description: This article details the deeply troubling ordeal of Umar Zameer, a Toronto accountant who was wrongly charged with first-degree murder after a tragic accident. In July 2021, while driving his family, ...

item.title: Lampard Confirms Onyeka Recovering for Super Eagles Friendlies
item.description: Frank Onyeka, the Super Eagles midfielder, don dey make good recovery from injury wey concern plenty people before di upcoming friendlies against Iran and Jordan. Coventry City manager Frank Lampard d...

item.title: Mufti Abubakar Zubeir urges Tanzanians to uphold peace during Eid al-Fitr celebrations
item.description: During Eid al-Fitr celebrations in Dar es Salaam, the Mufti of Tanzania, Sheikh Abubakar Zubeir, appealed to Muslims and all Tanzanians to continue protecting the country’s peace, describing it as the...

item.title: President Mwinyi Shares Eid al-Fitr Joy with Vulnerable Groups in Zanzibar
item.description: On March 21, 2026, the President of Zanzibar, Dr Hussein Ali Mwinyi, shared the spirit of Eid al-Fitr by providing support to hundreds of children, widows, and people with special needs. The event, he...

item.title: Triza Njeri Wishes Co-Wife Carol Kim Happy Birthday, Shows Maturity in Co-Parenting
item.description: Kikuyu influencer Triza Njeri stirred reactions online after wishing her estranged husband Karangu Muraya’s second wife, Carol Kim, a happy birthday. Previously, Triza and Karangu had openly fought on...

item.title: Raymond Chairman Gautam Singhania Suffers Minor Injuries In Maldives Speedboat Mishap
item.description: According to reports, Raymond Group chairman Gautam Singhania suffered injuries when a speedboat he was travelling in overturned in the Maldives. He was reportedly part of a private leisure outing wit...

item.title: Experts propose native British wildlife for new Bank of England banknotes
item.description: The Bank of England has announced that the next series of £5, £10, £20 and £50 notes will show native British wildlife, marking a shift away from featuring historical figures such as Winston Churchill...
